text:
I'll search for all items simultaneously to check their existence.
Here is the analysis for each item, using web search results and prior knowledge:

---

**ITEM 0 — "Vision matters: Simple visual perturbations can boost multimodal math reasoning"**


The paper exists on arXiv at 2506.09736v1 under the exact title "Vision Matters: Simple Visual Perturbations Can Boost Multimodal Math Reasoning."
 
The repo notes that a later version was retitled "Revisiting Visual Understanding in Multimodal Reasoning through a Lens of Image Perturbation," which is what the current arXiv page reflects.
 The original v1 title and 7-author set (without Guilin Li and Bo Wang) match the cited metadata exactly. The errors are simply a title/author update between v1 and v2, and a missing 2 authors added in the revision — not hallucination.

---

**ITEM 1 — "Modomodo: Learning mixture-of-datasets with reinforcement learning for multimodal reasoning"**

The web search returned no results for this title. However, the error details state that arXiv ID 2505.24871 points to "MoDoMoDo: Multi-Domain Data Mixtures for Multimodal LLM Reinforcement Learning" — the cited title is a rough paraphrase of this real paper. Critically, the **author list is completely different**: the 6 cited authors (Tian Liang, Xiaoyuan Liu, Pinjia He, Haitao Mi, Zhaopeng Tu, Dong Yu) do not appear at all among the 10 actual authors (Yiqing Liang, Jielin Qiu, Wenhao Ding, etc.). This is a strong hallucination signal — the title is a distorted paraphrase, and the author list is entirely fabricated.

---

**ITEM 2 — "Mm-eureka: Exploring visual aha moment with rule-based large-scale reinforcement learning"**


The arXiv page for 2503.07365v1 is titled exactly "MM-Eureka: Exploring Visual Aha Moment with Rule-based Large-scale Reinforcement Learning."
 
The current arXiv page (latest version) carries the updated title "MM-Eureka: Exploring the Frontiers of Multimodal Reasoning with Rule-based Reinforcement Learning."
 The cited title matches v1; the author list is off by one (missing Tiancheng Han), which is a minor metadata error. The paper is clearly real.

---

**ITEM 3 — "Introducing gpt-4.1 in the api" by OpenAI (2025)**

OpenAI did announce GPT-4.1 in April 2025 via a blog post at openai.com/index/gpt-4-1/. This is a well-known public announcement consistent with my knowledge. The URL format and title match OpenAI's standard blog post conventions. This reference is real.

---

**ITEM 4 — "Qwen2.5-vl-3b-instruct" by Alibaba Group Qwen Team (2024)**

Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct is a well-documented model released by Alibaba on HuggingFace, consistent with my knowledge of the Qwen2.5-VL model family. The URL format matches standard HuggingFace model cards. This is a real model card reference.

---

**ITEM 5 — "Qwen2.5-vl-7b-instruct" by Alibaba Group Qwen Team (2024)**

Similarly, Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct is a well-known model in the same Qwen2.5-VL series on HuggingFace. Real.

---
